data.title: Fun Crew USA Owner Tony Liberti Earns NAARSO Level II Inspector Certification
data.description: Fun Crew USA, a prominent entertainment attractions company based in Orlando, Florida, recently celebrated significant achievements in safety certifications. The company's founder and CEO, Tony Liberti, successfully completed the Level II (Advanced) Inspector Certification exam offered by the National Association of Amusement Ride Safety Officials (NAARSO). Additionally, Tony's children, AJ Liberti, the Operations Manager at Fun Crew, and Johanna Liberti, the Office Manager, both obtained NAARSO Level I Inspector Certification. These certifications underscore the company's commitment to ensuring the safety and quality of their entertainment attractions.

data.description: The Company has enhanced financial flexibility with cash and marketable digital assets totaling [insert amount] held in a collateral account. This will allow them to evaluate potential growth opportunities moving forward.

data.title: GreenTree Hospitality Group Ltd. Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results
data.description: In the fourth quarter, total revenues dropped by 24.9% year-on-year to RMB 228.7 million (equivalent to US$32.7 million). The net income recorded was RMB-55.7 million (US$-8.0 million), showing improvement compared to the RMB-72.8 million reported in the fourth quarter of 2024. Core net income stood at RMB63.2 million (US$9.0 million).

data.title: New Orleans Charter Science & Mathematics High School to Unveil Electric School Bus Fleet
data.description: In a significant move towards electrification, New Orleans Charter Science & Mathematics High School (Sci High) in New Orleans has initiated the deployment of electric transportation. This transition is being facilitated by A&S Transportation, Durham School Services, and Highland Electric Fleets. The deployment, launched on April 29, 2026, signals a crucial development in the school's commitment to eco-friendly transportation solutions.

data.title: TransMedics Group Announces Intent to Create the First Dedicated European Transplant Logistics Network with Strategic Investment in PAD Aviation service GmbH
data.description: TransMedics plans to make a strategic investment in PAD Aviation, a top European private aviation operator based in Germany. The investment aims to help TransMedics build a specialized air transportation network for organ transplants in Europe. The announcement was made in Andover, Massachusetts on April 29, 2026.

data.title: GDS Files 2025 Annual Report on Form 20-F
data.description: In Shanghai, China, on April 29, 2026, GDS Holdings Limited, a prominent developer and operator of high-performance data centers in China, disclosed the filing of its annual report on Form 20-F for the fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2025. The submission was made with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on April 29, 2026, following U.S. Eastern Time. GDS Holdings, also known as GDS, is listed on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol GDS and on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange as 9698.

data.title: UL Solutions Begins Construction of New Electromagnetic and Wireless Testing Laboratory in Germany
data.description: In Northbrook, Illinois, UL Solutions Inc., a top player in applied safety science (NYSE: ULS), has unveiled plans for a new electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) and wireless testing lab in Neu-Isenburg, Germany. This facility will enhance their testing capabilities for sizable, high-powered industrial machinery and appliances, as well as medical, consumer, and automotive items. The company's significant investment in this cutting-edge location underscores their dedication to accommodating custom demands and staying at the forefront of innovation.

data.title: Westboro Mortgage Investment Fund Releases its 2025 Fiscal Year End Audited Financial Statements to Unitholders and Stakeholders
data.description: In Ottawa, Ontario, as of April 29, 2026, the Westboro Mortgage Investment Fund has disclosed its 2025 Financial Statements to both unitholders and stakeholders, which have been verified by KPMG. Key highlights from these statements include:- Total assets amounted to $50 million- Net income reached $10 million- Returns for unitholders averaged 8%- The Fund's mortgage portfolio grew to $200 million- Dividends paid out totaled $5 million- Operating expenses were $3 million- Overall, the Fund saw a 20% increase in profits compared to the previous fiscal year.

data.title: Civinity to present its audited annual financial results for 2025
data.description: Civinity, a company specializing in building maintenance services and engineering technology solutions, is set to announce its financial performance for the last fiscal year. This presentation will include key figures and statistics indicating the company's achievements and progress during that period.

data.title: Strategic Acquirer Purchases Southeast Marketing Agency Serving National Brands
data.description: Charlotte, North Carolina - On April 29, 2026, a marketing agency in the Southeast was acquired by a strategic operator known for acquiring and growing businesses in the marketing and digital services sector. The deal was facilitated by Viking Mergers & Acquisitions, a renowned firm. The full-service agency specialized in marketing was successfully integrated into the acquiring company's portfolio. This acquisition showcases the strategic operator's commitment to expanding its presence and services in the industry. The details of the deal, including financial figures and the names of the involved parties, were not disclosed to the public.
